page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at a base clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the average tier across multiple workloads. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1562.49 and a multi-core score of 16538.21. Memory bandwidth tests show 927.07 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 64.29 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In database and key-value operations, the instance handles 15.27 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 5986.79 on PassMark database operations. For language model tasks, it processes 135M model prompts at 962.14 tokens per second, though performance drops to a weak 23.28 tokens per second when processing prompts for a larger 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.hfc7.4xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-memory profile for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local SSD storage. The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable execution times without resource contention. This hardware profile is well-suited for medium-scale web serving, batch processing, cryptography tasks leveraging its hardware-accelerated AES instructions, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the KVM hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance across the board, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 16538.21 and a Redis throughput of 15.27 million SET operations per second. While it handles smaller 135M parameter language models efficiently, performance drops significantly on larger 7B models. This instance is designed for CPU-intensive workloads, including static web serving, database operations, and moderate computational tasks that benefit from dedicated x86_64 execution units without requiring high memory density.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering reliable performance for CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 32 GB RAM.
tagline: A dedicated 16-core Intel Xeon compute instance optimized for high-frequency CPU workloads and balanced memory performance.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average performance in database operations and Redis key-value transactions
Suited for static web serving, batch processing, and lightweight inference
